Empires & Puzzles Armand Moncharmin Review: The Sleepy Strategist

Armand Moncharmin brings a unique twist to the battlefield with his Bodyguard Mega Minion that puts enemies to sleep. Discover how this Opera patron uses naps as a weapon and whether his stats still hold up in the current meta.
Armand Moncharmin: The Goblin Who Naps You to Death
Armand Moncharmin: The Goblin Patron Who Puts the "Rest" in "Rest in Peace"
Armand Moncharmin is a Legendary Nature hero from the
event. Released in late 2024, he brought a refreshing and comical twist to the often serious roster of heroes. As a

, he has the ability to bypass defensive buffs, which pairs nastily with his high-damage Mega Minion. His primary role is a disruptor and damage dealer who controls the battlefield by literally putting his enemies to sleep—a tactic usually reserved for boring lectures, but weaponized here with lethal efficiency. He doesn't have a costume yet, so this review focuses on his singular, dapper self.

Armand is a visual delight, breaking away from the typical "angry warrior screaming at the sky" trope. He depicts a wealthy, perhaps slightly shady, goblin patron of the arts. Dressed in a dashing purple suit with a top hat, monocle, and a cane topped with a massive diamond, he exudes an air of arrogant opulence. He's tossing cash with one hand and holding his cane with the other, clearly ready to buy his way out of trouble or beat you with his wealth. It's a whimsical, character-rich design that perfectly fits the theatrical theme of the Opera family.
When Armand was released, his stats were impressive. However, looking at the landscape in early 2026, he is showing his age. With a base power of
, he falls significantly behind the modern "Construct" and "Astral" behemoths that push 1260+ Power and 1300+ stats. While his limit-broken stats (LB2) of
help bridge the gap, he is no longer a stat monster. He relies heavily on his mechanics rather than raw numbers to stay competitive.
"Make Them Harmless" is a masterclass in tactical disruption.

Upon release, Armand was slightly overshadowed by his portal-mate Von Rothbart, but savvy players quickly realized the power of his "Sleep done right" mechanic. He became a staple in defenses for his beefy minion and annoying control.
His stats have undoubtedly crept downward. Modern hitters deal 450%+ to all or have higher base stats. However, the
mechanic scales with his stats (so troops and limit breaks help immensely) and remains a unique form of mitigation (the minion HP) and offense.
He is not the "God Tier" monster he might have been, but he is extremely effective in
(Very Fast speed makes him a nightmare) and
. In standard raids, he requires protection but can still win games if that Mega Minion gets going.
Armand benefits from keeping his Mega Minion alive and hitting hard.
Increases the Mega Minion's attack (250% of caster's attack is huge scaling) and HP (50% inheritance).
Slightly less defense for Armand himself.
Maximizes his bulk to ensure he fires.

path is recommended. The Mega Minion is his unique selling point; you want it to have as much HP as a tank and hit like a sniper.
The sleep mechanic is unique because it's applied
the turn by the minion, often locking an enemy down for their turn.
The Mega Minion provides a massive HP buffer (~1100+ HP), effectively doubling his lifespan.
+60% damage on sleeping targets can turn a scratch into a fatal wound.
He heals himself when ailments are removed.
Armand is a survivalist's dream attacker. He hits everyone, summons a personal tank that hits back, and shuts down enemies with naps.
Heroes who destroy minions (e.g., Grimsteel, Ogima, or newer Lunar heroes) or prevent minion summoning completely neuter him.
Sleep is broken by tile damage. In offense, an accidental cascade can wake the target immediately, wasting the effect.
In a fast meta, average speed can sometimes be too slow without mana support.
He needs Limit Breaks to survive the initial onslaught of modern 1300+ attack heroes.
He hates minion destroyers and clumsy tile play. If you can't control the board or protect his minion, he loses much of his value.
At Very Fast speed, he can summon his minion early and often, creating an impenetrable wall of HP and constantly sleeping enemies.

and misses its attack. Plus, the minion damage doesn't wake it.
Great for boss waves. The sleep allows you to control dangerous bosses while the minion whittles them down.
Phorcys buffs minion damage, making Armand's Mega Minion hit even harder.

While Mega Minions replace normal minions, having other minion summoners ensures that even if the Mega Minion dies, Armand has some cover. Note: Be careful not to overwrite his Mega Minion with a weaker one if you have another Mega Minion summoner (though you usually can only have one Mega Minion).
Two Armands means two Mega Minions. That's two enemies put to sleep every turn and double the passive damage.
(steals minions), and any hero with "Destroy Minion" capabilities.
(Damage over Time). If you apply a burn or poison, the tick damage
wake the enemy (though usually, it's direct damage, it's safer not to risk waking them if you want the mana freeze).
The card says "woken once it takes damage... excludes damage from minions and fiends". Usually DoT wakes them, so avoid pairing with heavy burn/poison if you rely on the sleep for control.

If you pulled Armand early, celebrate! He will carry you through almost all PvE content. His minion is essentially a 6th team member. Focus on leveling him and give him your best Ranger emblems. Don't worry too much about the sleep mechanic complexity; just enjoy the extra damage and survival.
Armand is a tactical pick. He won't nuke a modern defense in one shot, but in

, he is top-tier. If you have spare Alpha Aethers, he is a decent candidate for LB2 solely for the stat boost to his minion, but prioritize your main war defense and titan specialists first. He is a luxury hero who is fun and effective but not mandatory.
- The community immediately recognized that applying sleep via a minion at the end of the turn fixed the biggest issue with the ailment: accidental wake-ups.
- Early reviews noted that while he lived in Von Rothbart's shadow, he was the sleeper hit (pun intended) of the portal.
- Players discovered his ability to essentially "stun-lock" Titans and bosses by letting the minion put them to sleep turn after turn.

Armand Moncharmin is the epitome of "work smarter, not harder." While other heroes scream and smash, he simply summons a hired goon to do the dirty work while he counts his money. He has aged, but his unique Mega Minion mechanic keeps him relevant in specific niches like Rush wars and Titan control. If you want a hero who adds survivability, control, and a touch of class to your roster, Armand is your goblin.
A solid, fun, and mechanically unique hero who remains a
tactical option, especially when you need to force the enemy to take a nap.
A deep dive into Armand Moncharmin, the 5-star Nature hero from the Secrets of the Opera family. We analyze his stats, unique Mega Minion sleep mechanic, and modern viability in Empires & Puzzles.
